title: rosa × collina var. cerasifera
intro: Rosa × collina var. cerasifera, also known as the pink dog rose or fragrant dog rose, is a fragrant, deciduous shrub native to Europe and western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal remedies, where its roots and leaves are used to treat various health issues. The Rosa × collina var. cerasifera has been traditionally used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, and its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties are believed to provide health benefits, including reducing stress and improving skin health. Rosa × collina var. cerasifera can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
